messages.content:
proofread: 

Phase one
Hieronymus, the grand wizard of the Lanova Wizardry Center, has forgotten the location of the Chalice of Immortality. This has concerned himself and the council members, namely Madame Moulan and Nazradin the geomancer. Serjik, who has recently joined the council, is not very informed about the matter, but he tries to make himself aware in order to gain any advantage. Alethea, a former council member, is a sorceress who was trapped years ago in a magical painting by Nazradin Concpiricy due to her opposition to the council's policies regarding the chalice.

At first, Hieronymus is worried but does not show it. He doesn't want to see his memory, which has been his strong point, fail him. Since Nazradin, as the creator of the Apparatus, cannot determine the exact location of the Chalice in the Floating Lands, the council members ultimately decide, with Hieronymus' consent, to invite wizards from all over the world to Lanova in order to find the Chalice of Immortality. However, as the news of the Chalice's disappearance could cast doubt on Citadel, the legislative authority of magic, this invitation is arranged in the form of a trial in which volunteer wizards must get themselves to the Chalice to drink a dose of the immortality elixir as a reward.

Phase One:

Hieronymus, the Grand Wizard of the Lanova Wizardry Center, has forgotten the location of the Chalice of Immortality, which concerns him and other council members, namely Madame Moulan and Nazradin, the Geomancer.

Serjik, a new council member, is not well-informed on the matter but seeks to educate himself to gain an advantage. Alethea, a former council member and sorceress, was trapped in a magical painting by Nazradin Concpiricy years ago due to her opposition to the council's policies regarding the Chalice.

At first, Hieronymus is worried but conceals it, not wanting to see his memory, his strong point, fail him. Since Nazradin, creator of the Apparatus, cannot determine the precise location of the Chalice in the Floating Lands, the council members ultimately agree, with Hieronymus' consent, to invite wizards from across the world to Lanova to search for the Chalice of Immortality.

However, due to the potential doubt the Chalice's disappearance could cast on Citadel, the legislative authority of magic, this invitation is arranged in the form of a trial. Volunteer wizards must find the Chalice and drink a dose of the immortality elixir as a reward.

From the very beginning, the conflicts between the council members become apparent and the rumor of Chalice's disappearance becomes serious. The volunteers who are trying to find their place in the center gradually become aware of the purpose behind the trial and realize that the sole purpose of the trial is to find the chalice of Immortality that Hiranymus has forgotten.

However, as the volunteers progress through the trial, they uncover other secrets and hidden agendas among the council members.

Serjik, who initially joined the council to gain an advantage, becomes disillusioned with the council's ways and begins to question his loyalty to them. Alethea, still trapped in the magical painting, communicates with the volunteers through visions and warns them of the council members' true intentions.

As the trial progresses, it becomes clear that the council has been using the Chalice of Immortality for their own gain and is willing to sacrifice anything or anyone to maintain their power over magic.

The volunteers, led by Serjik and aided by Alethea's visions, work together to find the Chalice and put an end to the corrupted council's rule. Through their journey, they learn the true meaning of magic and the responsibility that comes with it.

In the end, the volunteers not only find the Chalice but also overthrow the corrupt council and establish a new order that upholds the principles of justice and equality.

proofread: From the very beginning, the conflicts between the council members become apparent and the rumor of Chalice's disappearance becomes serious. The volunteers who are trying to find their place in the center gradually become aware of the purpose behind the trial and realize that the sole purpose of the trial is to find the chalice of Immortality that Hiranymus has forgotten.

From the very beginning, the conflicts between the council members become apparent, and the rumor of the Chalice's disappearance becomes serious. The volunteers, who are trying to find their place in the center, gradually become aware of the purpose behind the trial and realize that the sole motive is to find the Chalice of Immortality that Hiranymus has forgotten.

proofread: The first time a player's avatar reaches the Chalice and returns it to the hub, the first game phase ends, and we enter the second phase with the chalice disappearing again. In fact, Hieronymus takes the Chalice to the Floating Lands again to hide it, but he doesn't remember where he has hidden it. In the meantime, Nazradin and Moulan, who has become suspicious of Hieronymus, almost become certain that he is lying to them about forgetting the location of the Chalice and that he only wants to prevent them from accessing it. With such doubts and suspicions, the conflicts gradually become more serious.

The first time a player's avatar reaches the Chalice and returns it to the hub, the first game phase ends, and we enter the second phase with the Chalice disappearing again. In fact, Hieronymus takes the Chalice to the Floating Lands again to hide it, but he doesn't remember where he has hidden it. In the meantime, Nazradin and Moulan, who have become suspicious of Hieronymus, almost become certain that he is lying to them about forgetting the location of the Chalice and is only preventing them from accessing it. With such doubts and suspicions, the conflicts gradually become more serious.

proofread: A new invite is issued, and the wizards descend once again toward Lanova. This time, at the request of Nazradin and accompanied by Moulan, the volunteers are allowed to establish themselves somewhere in the garden of the center. This is the beginning of the Brotherhood of Wizards. During this period, the volunteer wizards exit from anonymity and, in a way, acquire an identity. From now on, the avatars we choose each have a name. The first avatar to reach the Chalice and return it to the center drinks from it. This first avatar is Sura from the first game, which also brings the Brotherhood of Wizards into existence with his presence in the hub. He is somewhat positioned at the head of the Brotherhood.

A new invitation is issued, and the wizards once again descend upon Lanova. This time, at the request of Nazradin and accompanied by Moulan, the volunteers are allowed to establish themselves somewhere in the garden of the center. This marks the beginning of the Brotherhood of Wizards. During this period, the volunteer wizards come out of anonymity and acquire an identity. From now on, the avatars we choose have names. The first avatar to reach the Chalice and return it to the center drinks from it. This first avatar is Sura from the first game, who also brings the Brotherhood of Wizards into existence with his presence in the hub. He is somewhat positioned at the head of the Brotherhood.

proofread: Nazradin and Moulan gradually bring up the subject of replacing Hieronymus. Hieronymus, on the other hand, seems happier and more cheerful than before. It appears that he is unconsciously happy about not knowing where he has hidden the Chalice. He even implicitly welcomes the idea that a replacement will be found for him.

Nazradin and Moulan gradually bring up the subject of replacing Hieronymus. However, Hieronymus seems happier and more cheerful than before, despite this. It appears that he is unconsciously happy about not knowing where he has hidden the Chalice. He even implicitly welcomes the idea that a replacement will be found for him.

proofread: But as the matter becomes more serious and Nazradin and Moulan gradually bring up the topic of replacing Hieronymus, his behavior changes. The votes in the council regarding his replacement are evenly divided. Mulen and Nazradin are in favor, but Hieronymus himself, along with Sergik, who is trying to demonstrate loyalty to Hieronymus by supporting him, are opposed.

But as the matter becomes more serious and Nazradin and Moulan gradually discuss the topic of replacing Hieronymus, his behavior changes. The votes in the council regarding his replacement are evenly divided. Moulan and Nazradin are in favor, but Hieronymus himself, along with Serjik, who is trying to demonstrate loyalty to Hieronymus by supporting him, are opposed.

proofread: Everything here reaches a temporary deadlock. In fact, each time Hieronymus forgets the location of the chalice and an invitation is made to retrieve it from the floating lands. It seems like a new game has started for Hieronymus. In a way, he both possesses the ownership of the chalice and has become irresponsible towards it due to his forgetfulness. This limbo is pleasant for him, but it is worrisome for Moulan and Nazradin.

Everything here reaches a temporary deadlock. In fact, each time Hieronymus forgets the location of the Chalice, and an invitation is made to retrieve it from the Floating Lands, it seems like a new game has started for Hieronymus. In a way, he both possesses the ownership of the Chalice and has become irresponsible towards it due to his forgetfulness. While this limbo is pleasant for him, it is worrisome for Moulan and Nazradin.

proofread: This phase is accompanied by an increase in the main quests of the game, which serve as leverage to pressure Heranimus into entrusting the responsibility of the chalice and console to one of the volunteers.

With Molan and Nazradin's growing concerns, options are proposed to put pressure on Hieronymus. Nazradin believes that strengthening the Brotherhood, which consists of playable characters, not only grants them more power to find the chalice but also puts more pressure on Hieronymus. Nazradin suggests retrieving and repairing the magical wardrobe, Ambrogio, for the use of the volunteers. Sura, the leader of the Brotherhood, considers it a mission for the volunteers, but due to the scarcity of resources Nazradin has mentioned, the cost of this repair and maintenance is borne by the avatars.

This phase is accompanied by an increase in the main quests of the game, which serve as leverage to pressure Hieronymus into entrusting the responsibility of the Chalice and console to one of the volunteers.

As Moulan and Nazradin grow increasingly concerned, options are proposed to put pressure on Hieronymus. Nazradin believes that strengthening the Brotherhood, which consists of playable characters, not only grants them more power to find the Chalice but also puts more pressure on Hieronymus. Nazradin suggests retrieving and repairing the magical wardrobe, Ambrogio, for the use of the volunteers. Sura, the leader of the Brotherhood, considers it a mission for the volunteers. However, due to the scarcity of resources, the cost of repairing and maintaining Ambrogio is borne by the avatars.

proofread: Moulan, who is seeking a way to hold an election for Hieronymus' successor, despite Nazradin's pessimism, concludes that they must officialize Serjik's temporary membership in the console. By doing so, they can gain Serjik's favor and obtain his positive vote for Heranimus' succession. To achieve this, he writes a letter to a wizard named Celestophon, who resides in Citadel and has a reputation for corruption, requesting him to introduce Serjik as the representative of Citadel. Celestophon, in response to Moulan, demands a fee for this task. Finally, Moulan places the expenses, like in previous cases, on the volunteers.

Moulan, who is seeking a way to hold an election for Hieronymus' successor, concludes that they must officialize Serjik's temporary membership in the council. By doing so, they can gain Serjik's favor and obtain his positive vote for Hieronymus' succession. To achieve this, she writes a letter to a wizard named Celestophon, who resides in Citadel and has a reputation for corruption, requesting him to introduce Serjik as the representative of Citadel. Celestophon, in response to Moulan, demands a fee for this task. Finally, Moulan places the expenses, as in previous cases, on the volunteers.

proofread: Jeila, a descendant of Madame Mullen, who has arrived at the center after a long time, is supposed to assist Moulan and the council in the trial. As a clumsy and emotional wizard, she faces problems with her great-grandmother. Jeila, who is fascinated by Alethea ultimately succeeds, with the help of Sura and without Nazradin and Mullen's agreements, in freeing the sorceress from the enchantment of the painting. Despite her fear of Alethea's liberation, Nazradin, who had a role in the sorceress's captivity, ultimately accepts her presence as a leverage against Hieronymus. He even helps the sorceress gain more power in the third phase.

Jeila, a descendant of Madame Moulan, arrives at the center after a long time and is supposed to assist Moulan and the council in the trial. As a clumsy and emotional wizard, she encounters problems with her great-grandmother. Jeila becomes fascinated by Alethea and ultimately succeeds, with the help of Sura and without the agreement of Nazradin and Moulan, in freeing the sorceress from the enchantment of the painting. Despite her fear of Alethea's liberation, Nazradin, who had a role in the sorceress's captivity, ultimately accepts her presence as leverage against Hieronymus. He even helps the sorceress gain more power in the third phase.

proofread: Here, as soon as the conditions for two of the three main quests mentioned above are fulfilled, which are provided through the narrative resource designed in the game, the pressure on Hieronymus to relinquish his power increases, and the goal of the game changes from finding the chalice for Hieronymus to determining a successor for him. Therefore, any avatar that reaches the chalice will be introduced as Hieronymus' successor and will be placed at the head of the Lanova Wizard's Console.

As soon as the conditions for two of the three main quests mentioned above are fulfilled, which are provided through the narrative resource designed in the game, the pressure on Hieronymus to relinquish his power increases. The goal of the game changes from finding the Chalice for Hieronymus to determining a successor for him. Therefore, any avatar that reaches the Chalice will be introduced as Hieronymus' successor and will be placed at the head of the Lanova Wizard's Council.

proofread: Phase Two can occur anywhere in the game. in this phase, Hieronymus is not as joyful as in Phase One. He sees his power lost and finds himself caught between madness and rebellion. These conditions prevent the initial goal of this phase from being satisfied. In fact, as soon as the first avatar reaches the chalice, Hieronymus transforms them into a toad, takes the chalice, and flees to Xaosia, where he had originally found the chalice. The true motive behind Hieronymus taking the chalice to Xaosia is revealed later in the final phase of the game. However, what is clear is that the goal of the game changes once again. Now, the objective is to retrieve the chalice from Hieronymus' grasp.

Phase Two can occur anywhere in the game. In this phase, Hieronymus is not as joyful as in Phase One. He sees his power lost and finds himself caught between madness and rebellion. These conditions prevent the initial goal of this phase from being satisfied. As soon as the first avatar reaches the Chalice, Hieronymus transforms them into a toad and flees to Xaosia, where he had originally found the Chalice. The true motive behind Hieronymus taking the Chalice to Xaosia is revealed later in the final phase of the game. However, what is clear is that the goal of the game changes once again. Now, the objective is to retrieve the Chalice from Hieronymus' grasp.

proofread: In this phase, if the quest to release Alethea from the painting curse has been completed, it is possible to count on her assistance in defeating Heranimus by empowering her. By doing so, Althea regains some of her power and helps strengthen the volunteers.
Similarly, if the Quest for Empowering the Brotherhood is fulfilled to strengthen the volunteers, another quest arises to further empower Sura and the Brotherhood. In this quest, Sura becomes an official member of the Wizard's Console. This event implicitly strengthens the volunteers against Hieronymus.

Also, Serjik, in exchange for becoming the head of the council, will provide the volunteers with the lost techniques of Bartholomew the Wizard.

Note: If the initial nodes of each of the main quests are not completed, it will still proceed to Phase Three. Similarly, if the quests are not completed in this phase, they will be transferred to the next phase. In fact, the quests will be designed in a way that they can be undertaken in each phase, except in Phase Two, where at least two of them must be completed to change the game's objective.

In this phase, if the quest to release Alethea from the painting curse has been completed, it is possible to count on her assistance in defeating Hieronymus by empowering her. By doing so, Alethea regains some of her power and helps to strengthen the volunteers.

Similarly, if the Quest for Empowering the Brotherhood to strengthen the volunteers is fulfilled, another quest arises to further empower Sura and the Brotherhood. In this quest, Sura becomes an official member of the Wizard's Council. This event implicitly strengthens the volunteers against Hieronymus.

Additionally, in exchange for becoming the head of the council, Serjik will provide the volunteers with the lost techniques of Bartholomew the Wizard.

Note: If the initial nodes of each of the main quests are not completed, it will still proceed to Phase Three. Similarly, if the quests are not completed in this phase, they will be transferred to the next phase. The quests will be designed in a way that they can be undertaken in each phase, except in Phase Two, where at least two of them must be completed to change the game's objective.
